behaviors.description: Keyword-frequency analyzer assigns correct sentiment for reviews containing only configured positive/negative words.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit beats Integration because the failure boundary is the analyzer’s local counting/threshold logic, not cross-module handoff (MOCK-MISS=no). Unit then beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA=no). Higher levels were rejected: Integration is disqualified by behavior text focusing on one analyzer’s decision rule; System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because there is no real infra dependency and no AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the keyword-frequency analyzer function with controlled review text and config fixtures (word lists + thresholds + tie params); assert exact sentiment label outcomes for representative positive-only and negative-only inputs.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer produces reasoning that cites specific phrases from the source review instead of generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit beats Integration on MOCK-MISS (core check is output quality, not module wiring), Unit beats System on REAL-INFRA (infra is not the target), but Agentic beats prior winner because correctness depends on real model judgment over language grounding (LLM-DEP=yes; mocks would hide failure). Workflow is rejected by behavior text: this is a single explanation-generation step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on reviews with distinctive phrases; assert the explanation includes phrase-level grounding from the input (or close paraphrases tied to concrete spans) and fails when output is generic/non-specific.

behaviors.description: For mixed-signal reviews (both positive and negative keywords), sentiment score follows configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit beats Integration because the failure is in deterministic tie-break logic inside the keyword analyzer, not a module boundary (MOCK-MISS=no). Unit beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess is needed (REAL-INFRA=no). Higher levels were rejected: behavior text is purely about configured rule application, which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke analyzer with mixed-keyword inputs that produce ties under different threshold configs; assert the returned sentiment exactly matches each configured tie-break policy.

behaviors.description: Explanation for sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") identifies negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit beats Integration (not primarily a handoff bug), Unit beats System (infra not core), then Agentic wins because the behavior is semantic intent detection in generated reasoning, which depends on real model output quality (LLM-DEP=yes; deterministic stubs can’t expose sarcasm failures). Workflow is rejected by behavior text: one agentic interpretation task, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run real explanation generation on a sarcasm-focused set; assert explanations explicitly indicate negative intent and tie it to sarcastic phrasing rather than literal positive tokens.

behaviors.description: Score and explanation are returned together in one response, and explanation aligns with the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Integration beats Unit because this behavior is cross-module orchestration/consistency between two analyzers (MOCK-MISS=yes for handoff). System does not beat Integration because real OS/network/subprocess is not inherently required (REAL-INFRA=no). Agentic beats Integration per Integration-brake (d): one model decision governs whether explanation direction matches score; mocking the explainer can hide real contradiction (LLM-DEP=yes). Workflow is rejected because only one agentic decision is needed; the score path is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end service call with real explanation analyzer enabled; assert single payload contains both fields and that explanation polarity/wording is directionally consistent with computed score across positive, negative, and neutral cases.
